chemotherapy_drugs.special_instructions: First infusion over 90 minutes; if well tolerated second infusion over 60 minutes; if well tolerated subsequent infusions over 30 minutes. Dose banded according to CSCCN agreed bands.

dose_modifications.notes: Consider blood transfusion or erythropoietin if patient symptomatic of anaemia or has a haemoglobin of less than 8g/dL. There is little need to adjust the dose of bevacizumab for haematological toxicity.
No information available for hepatic impairment (n/a).
No information available for renal impairment (n/a).

dose_modifications.condition: NCI-CTC grade 3 and above non-haematological toxicity
dose_modifications.notes: Delay treatment until adverse effect has resolved to NCI-CTC grade 1 or below.

dose_modifications.condition: Gastrointestinal perforation, arterial thromboembolic event, NCI-CTC grade 3+ haemorrhagic event, NCI-CTC grade 3+ congestive heart failure, NCI-CTC grade 4 fistula
dose_modifications.notes: Bevacizumab should be stopped. Doses should be omitted and not reduced for adverse reactions. If more than two doses are missed due to adverse events treatment should be stopped.

dose_modifications.condition: NCI-CTC grade 3 and above persistent hypertension
dose_modifications.notes: For NCI-CTC grade 3 and above hypertension that is persistent consider stopping treatment.
Omit bevacizumab if proteinuria >2g/24h. Repeat 24 hour urine collection prior to next dose. If <2g/24h administer bevacizumab and continue 24h urine collection until protein is 1g/24h or less.

treatment_delay_criteria.notes: No specific haematological threshold defined for bevacizumab. Delay for NCI-CTC grade 3+ non-haematological toxicity until resolved to grade 1 or below.

monitoring_requirements: FBC, LFTs and U&Es prior to each cycle
EDTA or calculated creatinine clearance prior to each cycle
Blood pressure and dipstick urinalysis for proteinuria prior to treatment with bevacizumab
special_warnings: This protocol may require funding. This is an unlicensed indication for bevacizumab.
Half life of bevacizumab is approximately twenty days.
Bevacizumab – neutral (extravasation risk classification).
Gastric protection with a proton pump inhibitor or H2 antagonist may be considered in patients at high risk of GI ulceration or bleed.
Six cycles will be set in Aria.

drugs.special_instructions: First infusion over 90 minutes; if well tolerated second infusion over 60 minutes; if well tolerated subsequent infusions over 30 minutes. Dose banded according to CSCCN agreed bands.
drugs.notes: once per cycle | First infusion over 90 minutes; if well tolerated second infusion over 60 minutes; if well tolerated subsequent infusions over 30 minutes. Dose banded according to CSCCN agreed bands.

indication: Third line treatment of glioblastoma multiforme; WHO Performance status 0, 1, 2

chemotherapy_drugs.special_instructions: Dose banded per national dose bands (10mg/ml). Maximum dose for AUC 5 is 750mg (creatinine clearance capped at 125ml/min); set at 790mg in ARIA to comply with national dose bands. Dose may need alteration if renal function changes by >10% between cycles. AUC 4 may be considered with EDTA clearance – seek consultant advice.
Dose banded per national dose bands (20mg/ml).

pre_medications.timing: 15-30 minutes prior to chemotherapy on day 1
pre_medications.notes: Oral or intravenous. Patients on daily dexamethasone ≥8mg need no extra antiemetic dexamethasone. Patients on <8mg/day: increase to 8mg daily on days 1-4 of cycle only.

pre_medications.timing: 15-30 minutes prior to chemotherapy on day 1

take_home_medicines.frequency: twice daily for 3 days starting day 2
take_home_medicines.notes: Take 4mg twice a day for 3 days starting on day 2 of the cycle. Adjust per existing dexamethasone dose.

take_home_medicines.frequency: three times a day when required
take_home_medicines.notes: Please supply 28x10mg tablets or nearest equivalent pack size.

take_home_medicines.frequency: twice daily for 3 days starting evening of day 1
take_home_medicines.notes: Take 8mg twice a day for three days starting on the evening of day one of the cycle.

dose_modifications.notes: Neutrophil equal to or more than 1.5x10^9/L required prior to day one of cycle one.
Platelets equal to or more than 100x10^9/L required prior to day one of cycle one.

Delay treatment for seven days. If counts recover continue at initial dose. If counts remain low continue with treatment using 80% of last dose. If myelosuppression recurs despite dose reduction stop treatment.
Continue with treatment using 80% of the last dose if counts remain low after 7 day delay.
Delay treatment for seven days. If counts recover continue at initial dose. If counts still fall within this range continue using same dose.
If platelet level falls below 50x10^9/L reduce the dose by 50%.
Etoposide: bilirubin 26-51 µmol/L or AST/ALT 60-180 units: give 50% dose.
Etoposide: bilirubin >51 µmol/L or AST/ALT >180 units: clinical decision required.
Carboplatin: do not use if creatinine clearance < 20 ml/min. Changes in GFR of more than 10% between cycles may require dose adjustment.
Etoposide: creatinine clearance 15-50 ml/min give 75% dose.
Etoposide: creatinine clearance < 15 ml/min give 50% dose.

treatment_delay_criteria.notes: Neutrophil ≥1.5x10^9/L and platelets ≥100x10^9/L required prior to day 1 of cycle 1.

monitoring_requirements: EDTA or calculated creatinine clearance before the 1st cycle
FBC, LFTs and U&Es prior to each cycle
special_warnings: Carboplatin – irritant (extravasation risk).
Etoposide – irritant (extravasation risk).
Maximum dose of carboplatin for AUC 5 is 750mg; set at 790mg in ARIA to comply with national dose bands.
Prophylactic antibiotics can be considered if required.
Gastric protection with a proton pump inhibitor or H2 antagonist may be considered in patients at high risk of GI ulceration or bleed.
Patients are often on long-term dexamethasone – adjust antiemetic dexamethasone accordingly.

indication: Adjuvant treatment for medulloblastoma; Performance status 0, 1, 2

pre_medications.timing: Prior to vincristine on days 8 and 15
pre_medications.notes: Oral or intravenous on days 8 and 15.

take_home_medicines.frequency: once on day 1 of each cycle

once daily for 3 days starting day 2

dose_modifications.notes: Neutrophil equal to or more than 1x10^9/L required prior to starting chemotherapy treatment.
Platelets equal to or more than 100x10^9/L required prior to starting chemotherapy treatment.
Delay chemotherapy for at least one week.

dose_modifications.condition: < 0.5 x10^9/L or platelets < 100 x10^9/L for more than 2 weeks
dose_modifications.notes: If lack of count recovery after more than two weeks (neutrophils < 0.5x10^9/L or platelets < 100x10^9/L): give cisplatin and vincristine but omit lomustine for that course.

dose_modifications.condition: Recovered prior to next course after lomustine omission
dose_modifications.notes: Reintroduce lomustine at a reduced dose of 50mg/m2.

dose_modifications.condition: < 0.5 x10^9/L and neutropenic fever at any time (nadir)
dose_modifications.notes: Reduce lomustine to 50mg/m2 in the next course and all subsequent courses.

dose_modifications.condition: Further episode of neutropenia and fever (< 0.05 x10^9/L)
dose_modifications.notes: Reduce cisplatin to 50mg/m2 in the next and subsequent courses.
Reduce lomustine to 50mg/m2 in the next course and all subsequent courses.
Omit lomustine in the next course and all subsequent courses.
Vincristine: bilirubin 26-51 µmol/L or AST/ALT 60-180 units: give 50%.
Vincristine: bilirubin >51 µmol/L and normal AST/ALT: give 50%.
Vincristine: bilirubin >51 µmol/L and AST/ALT >180 units: omit.

dose_modifications.condition: > 25 µmol/L and AST/ALT > 5xULN, or bilirubin > 85 µmol/L
dose_modifications.notes: Lomustine: bilirubin >25 µmol/L and AST/ALT >5xULN: omit or dose reduce. Bilirubin >85 µmol/L or AST/ALT >5xULN: omit.
Creatinine clearance 45-60 ml/min: give 75% dose for both lomustine and cisplatin.
Creatinine clearance 30-45 ml/min: lomustine 50% dose. Cisplatin: less than 45 ml/min consider use of carboplatin.
Creatinine clearance < 30 ml/min: lomustine not recommended.
Substitute carboplatin 400mg/m2 for cisplatin at ototoxicity grade 2.
Omit cisplatin for ototoxicity grade 3-4.
Omit vincristine during current course and reduce to 1mg/m2 (maximum 2mg) for next course. If seizures or ileus do not recur, return to full dose. Rule out SIADH as cause of seizures.

dose_modifications.condition: Paraesthesia, weakness, abdominal pain or constipation from vincristine
dose_modifications.notes: Omit next vincristine dose; on recovery reintroduce at reduced dose of 1mg/m2 (max 2mg) increasing to full dose if symptoms do not return.

treatment_delay_criteria.notes: Neutrophil ≥1x10^9/L and platelets ≥100x10^9/L required prior to starting chemotherapy.

monitoring_requirements: FBC, LFTs and U&Es prior to day one of each cycle
Audiometry monitoring during treatment – fundamental and mandatory part of clinical practice
Patients should drink at least 3 litres of fluid in 24 hours after cisplatin administration
special_warnings: NPSA/2008/RRR001 must be followed for oral lomustine.
NPSA/2008/RRR04 must be followed for IV vinca alkaloid administration.
Ototoxicity monitoring using Brock/CTC (SIOP) grading is mandatory with cisplatin.
Growth factor (G-CSF) required per local formulary: filgrastim 30million units SC days 3-9, or lenograstim 33.6million units SC days 3-9, or pegfilgrastim 6mg SC day 2.
Gastric protection with PPI or H2 antagonist may be considered in high-risk patients.
Lomustine available as 40mg capsules – dose rounded to nearest 40mg.

Delay therapy until counts recover. If more than one delay necessary due to neutropenia or thrombocytopenia, reduce procarbazine duration from 10 days to 7 days. Haematological toxicity of lomustine may be cumulative.
Lomustine: bilirubin >25 µmol/L and AST/ALT >180: omit or dose reduce.
Procarbazine: bilirubin >50 µmol/L and normal AST/ALT: give 50%.
Procarbazine: bilirubin >85 µmol/L or AST/ALT >180: omit.
Lomustine: CrCl 45-60 ml/min give 75%.
Lomustine: CrCl 30-45 ml/min give 50%.
Lomustine: CrCl < 30 ml/min not recommended.
Procarbazine: serum creatinine >177 µmol/L give 50% dose. Not recommended with severe renal failure (CrCl < 10 ml/min).
Stop procarbazine immediately. Procarbazine should then be omitted from future cycles.

Procarbazine is a weak MAO inhibitor – dietary restrictions: avoid mature cheeses, yeast/meat extracts (Marmite, Oxo, Bovril), broad bean pods, pickled herring, salami, flavoured textured vegetable protein, over-ripe fruit, alcoholic drinks (especially heavy red wines such as Chianti), non-alcoholic beers/lagers/wines, foods that are fermented, pickled, smoked, hung or matured, miso soup and large amounts of soy sauce.
Lomustine available as 40mg capsules – dose rounded to nearest 40mg.
Procarbazine available as 50mg capsules – dose rounded to nearest 25mg with alternate day dosing.

indication: First line treatment of newly diagnosed glioblastoma multiforme starting four weeks after treatment with concomitant radiotherapy and temozolomide; Recurrent or progressive malignant glioma in patients previously treated with chemotherapy; Performance status 0, 1

chemotherapy_drugs.special_instructions: Cycle 1: 150mg/m2 once daily days 1-5 (maximum 300mg). At start of cycle 2 escalate to 200mg/m2 if NCI-CTC non-haematological toxicity ≤grade 2 (except alopecia, nausea, vomiting), neutrophils ≥1.5x10^9/L, and platelets ≥100x10^9/L. If not escalated at cycle 2 do not escalate subsequently. Dose banded per national dose bands. Capped at 2m2. Take on empty stomach, swallow whole, do not chew.

chemotherapy_drugs.frequency: once daily days 1-5 (cycles 2-6 if escalation criteria met)
chemotherapy_drugs.special_instructions: Cycles 2-6: 200mg/m2 (maximum 400mg) if escalation criteria met at start of cycle 2. Take on empty stomach, swallow whole, do not chew.

take_home_medicines.frequency: once daily for 5 days (cycle 1)
take_home_medicines.notes: Take on empty stomach, swallow whole, do not chew.

take_home_medicines.frequency: once daily 15-30 minutes prior to temozolomide
take_home_medicines.notes: An additional 8mg dose may be taken 12 hours later if required. Please supply original pack (10 tablets) or nearest appropriate equivalent.

take_home_medicines.frequency: once daily on Monday, Wednesday and Friday until lymphocytes above 0.8

dose_modifications.notes: Neutrophil equal to or more than 1.5x10^9/L required prior to starting treatment.
Platelets equal to or more than 100x10^9/L required prior to starting treatment.
Reduce dose by 50mg/m2. Temozolomide can be restarted provided neutrophils recover to ≥1x10^9/L within 7 days of planned date.
Discontinue temozolomide if neutrophils < 0.5x10^9/L. Also discontinue if dose of 100mg/m2 still results in unacceptable toxicity.
Reduce dose by 50mg/m2. Temozolomide can be restarted provided platelets recover to ≥100x10^9/L within 7 days.
Discontinue temozolomide if platelets < 10x10^9/L.
Stop temozolomide if bilirubin > 1.5xULN.
Stop temozolomide if ALT > 2.5xULN.
No dose reductions are required. Caution in severe renal impairment.

treatment_delay_criteria.notes: Temozolomide can be restarted if neutrophils recover to ≥1x10^9/L and platelets ≥100x10^9/L within 7 days of planned date. Complete blood count obtained on day 22 (21 days after first dose).

monitoring_requirements: FBC, LFTs, U&Es and glucose on day 22 of the cycle
Clinical examination including neurological assessment, whole brain imaging and chest x-ray prior to starting treatment
special_warnings: NPSA/2008/RRR001 must be followed for oral temozolomide.
Treatment is started four weeks after completing the concomitant chemoradiation phase.
Dose banded per national dose bands; capped at 2m2 (maximum 300mg cycle 1, 400mg cycles 2-6).
Temozolomide to be discontinued if dose of 100mg/m2 still results in unacceptable toxicity or same NCI-CTC grade 3 non-haematological toxicity recurs after dose reduction.
Gastric protection with PPI or H2 antagonist may be considered in high-risk patients.

If not escalated at cycle 2 do not escalate subsequently. Dose banded per national dose bands. Capped at 2m2. Take on empty stomach, swallow whole, do not chew.
drugs.notes: once daily days 1-5 (cycle 1 only) | Cycle 1: 150mg/m2 once daily days 1-5 (maximum 300mg). At start of cycle 2 escalate to 200mg/m2 if NCI-CTC non-haematological toxicity ≤grade 2 (except alopecia, nausea, vomiting), neutrophils ≥1.5x10^9/L, and platelets ≥100x10^9/L. If not escalated at cycle 2 do not escalate subsequently. Dose banded per national dose bands. Capped at 2m2. Take on empty stomach, swallow whole, do not chew. | CYCLE 1 DOSE (150mg/m²): dose-escalation cycle. Two Temozolomide entries exist: 150mg/m² for cycle 1 only, 200mg/m² for cycles 2-6 if escalation criteria met — both are intentional, representing different cycle doses.

drugs.frequency: once daily days 1-5 (cycles 2-6 if escalation criteria met)
drugs.special_instructions: Cycles 2-6: 200mg/m2 (maximum 400mg) if escalation criteria met at start of cycle 2. Take on empty stomach, swallow whole, do not chew.
drugs.notes: once daily days 1-5 (cycles 2-6 if escalation criteria met) | Cycles 2-6: 200mg/m2 (maximum 400mg) if escalation criteria met at start of cycle 2. Take on empty stomach, swallow whole, do not chew. | CYCLES 2-6 DOSE (200mg/m²): escalated dose if cycle 1 tolerated. Two Temozolomide entries exist: 150mg/m² for cycle 1 only, 200mg/m² for cycles 2-6 if escalation criteria met — both are intentional, representing different cycle doses.

indication: Recurrent or progressive malignant glioblastoma multiforme in patients not previously treated with chemotherapy; Performance status 0, 1

chemotherapy_drugs.special_instructions: Dose banded per national dose bands. Capped at 2m2 (maximum 400mg). Take on empty stomach, swallow whole, do not chew.

take_home_medicines.frequency: once daily for 5 days
take_home_medicines.notes: Take on empty stomach, swallow whole, do not chew.

take_home_medicines.frequency: once daily 15-30 minutes prior to temozolomide
take_home_medicines.notes: An additional 8mg may be taken 12 hours later if required. Please supply original pack (10 tablets) or nearest appropriate equivalent.

take_home_medicines.frequency: once daily on Monday, Wednesday and Friday until lymphocytes above 0.8

dose_modifications.notes: Neutrophil equal to or more than 1.5x10^9/L required prior to starting treatment.
Platelets equal to or more than 100x10^9/L required prior to starting treatment.
Reduce dose by 50mg/m2. Can be restarted if neutrophils recover to ≥1x10^9/L within 7 days.
Discontinue temozolomide.
Reduce dose by 50mg/m2. Can be restarted if platelets recover to ≥100x10^9/L within 7 days.
Discontinue temozolomide.
Stop temozolomide if bilirubin > 1.5xULN.
Stop temozolomide if ALT > 2.5xULN.
No dose reductions are required. Caution in severe renal impairment.

treatment_delay_criteria.notes: Temozolomide can be restarted if neutrophils recover to ≥1x10^9/L and platelets ≥100x10^9/L within 7 days of planned date. Complete blood count obtained on day 22 (21 days after first dose).

monitoring_requirements: FBC, LFTs, U&Es and glucose on day 22 of the cycle
Clinical examination including neurological assessment, whole brain imaging and chest x-ray prior to starting treatment (in recurrent disease)
special_warnings: NPSA/2008/RRR001 must be followed for oral temozolomide.
Dose banded per national dose bands; capped at 2m2 (maximum 400mg).
Temozolomide to be discontinued if dose of 100mg/m2 still results in unacceptable toxicity.
Gastric protection with PPI or H2 antagonist may be considered in high-risk patients.

indication: First line treatment of newly diagnosed glioblastoma multiforme in conjunction with radiotherapy; Performance status 0, 1

chemotherapy_drugs.frequency: once daily days 1-7 continuously throughout radiotherapy (maximum 49 days)
chemotherapy_drugs.special_instructions: Maximum 150mg; set at 145mg in ARIA to comply with national dose bands. Dose capped at 2m2. Started on first day of radiotherapy; stopped on last day of radiotherapy. Taken continuously including weekends. Maximum 49 days of treatment. Taken within 3 hours of radiotherapy. Take on empty stomach, swallow whole, do not chew.

take_home_medicines.frequency: once daily for 7 days per cycle (continuously throughout radiotherapy)
take_home_medicines.notes: Take on empty stomach, swallow whole, do not chew. Maximum 49 days total.

take_home_medicines.frequency: once daily 15-30 minutes prior to temozolomide
take_home_medicines.notes: An additional 8mg may be taken 12 hours later if required. Please supply original pack (10 tablets) or nearest appropriate equivalent.

take_home_medicines.frequency: three times a day when required (cycle 1 only)
take_home_medicines.notes: Please supply 30 tablets or nearest original pack size.

take_home_medicines.frequency: once daily on Monday, Wednesday and Friday
take_home_medicines.notes: Please supply sufficient for 49 days of treatment.

dose_modifications.notes: Neutrophil equal to or more than 1.5x10^9/L required prior to starting treatment.
Platelets equal to or more than 100x10^9/L required prior to starting treatment.
Interrupt temozolomide if neutrophils 0.5-1.5x10^9/L.
Discontinue temozolomide if neutrophils < 0.5x10^9/L.
Interrupt temozolomide if platelets 10-100x10^9/L.
Discontinue temozolomide if platelets < 10x10^9/L.
Stop temozolomide if bilirubin > 1.5xULN.
Stop temozolomide if ALT > 2.5xULN.
No dose reductions are required. Caution in severe renal impairment.

treatment_delay_criteria.notes: Neutrophil ≥1.5x10^9/L and platelets ≥100x10^9/L required to start. Interrupt if neutrophils 0.5-1.5x10^9/L or platelets 10-100x10^9/L during treatment. FBC monitored every 7 days.

monitoring_requirements: FBC, LFTs, U&Es and glucose prior to starting treatment and every seven days thereafter

special_warnings: NPSA/2008/RRR001 must be followed for oral temozolomide.
Temozolomide should be started on first day of radiotherapy and stopped on last day. Taken continuously even on days when radiotherapy is not given (e.g. weekends).
Maximum number of days of treatment is 49.
Temozolomide should be taken within three hours of radiotherapy.
Dose banded per national dose bands; maximum 145mg in ARIA (cap at 2m2).
Gastric protection with PPI or H2 antagonist may be considered in high-risk patients.
